uax:Description.uax:Text: This structure contains the Calibration information. It is used as an input argument in SetCalibration method. Note: The input data sent in SetCalibration shall be updated in the respective parameters of the asset under Maintenance/Calibration.
It is the date when the last calibration took place. Note: It can have the initial operation date for a new asset if it is not available.
It is the location where the last calibration took place.
It is the date of the next planned calibration.
It is the actual measured value of the calibration.
It is the nominal scale of the sensor. It corresponds also with the measurement range of the sensor.

It contains the URI of a certificate of the calibration target in case the calibration target is certified and the information available. Otherwise, the Variable should be omitted. The String shall be a URI as defined by RFC 3986. Example: MCE test document.
It defines the engineering unit of the value.
This structure provides the design value for a given physical quantity. It is used in JointDesignDataType.
It is the physical quantity of the value. Example: Force, Angle, etc.
It is the name of the given value.

It is the design value. The data type can be any simple data type such as Integer, String, Double, DateTime, etc. If the value is corresponding to the physical quantity, then the data type should be Double.
It is the engineering unit of the design value.
This structure provides the identification data for a given entity in the system.
It is the name of the entity identifier. Examples: VIN, Program Id, etc. Note: Application can send custom names which could be a combination of different terms in the system.
It is the description of the entity. Any additional information for the entity can be sent using this property. Examples: Name of the program in the given system.
It is the identifier of the given entity. Examples: JoiningProcessId, JointId, etc.
It is the origin identifier of the given entity. It is provided only when it is applicable and available for a given entity. Examples: JoiningProcessOriginId, JointOriginId, etc.
It indicates if the EntityId is provided by the external system or not. Example: VIN is provided by an external system.

It provides the type of the Entity. It has the following pre-defined values which shall be used for each instance of this type. Important Note: EntityType >=0 is defined as part of this specification. For application specific extensions, EntityType < 0 can be used.
This structure represents the errors occurred in the system which are outside the boundaries of the given program.
It is the classification of type of errors due to external factors. Examples: Tool Trigger Lost is Operator error. Temperature overheat is hardware error, etc.

It is the system-wide unique identifier of the error in the system. This will be useful if system wants to provide an identifier where user can query the system and get more information for troubleshooting. It can also point to the respective EventId reported if available.
It is the application or system specific error code.
It is user readable text message to describe the error.

This structure provides the base container for any joining process in a joining system. Note: This specification defines the meta data of a JoiningProcess, and the actual content of the Joining Process is application specific.
It is the meta data of the joining process.

It is the actual content of the joining process which is defined by JoiningProcessMetaData.Classification property. Examples: Joining Program, Joining Job, etc. Note: The content is application-specific and is not defined in this version of the specification.
This structure contains the identification information of a Joining Process. It is used in set of methods defined in JoiningProcessManagementType.
It is the system-wide unique identifier of the joining process.

It is an identifier to track the changes made to the original instance in the system.
Example: It can point to the identifier of the object when it was created for the first time. It should be an existing ID in the system if it is available else it can be NULL if it is not tracked in the system.
It is the configured selection name of the joining process in the system.
This structure provides the meta data which describes the joining process.
It is the system-wide unique identifier of the joining process.

It is an identifier to track the changes made to the original instance in the system. Example: It can point to the identifier of the object when it was created for the first time. It should be an existing ID in the system if it is available else it can be NULL if it is not tracked in a joining system.
It is the time when the joining process was created.
It is the time when the joining process was updated last time. If it is not available, it can be same as CreationTime.
It is the name of the joining process.
It is the description of the joining process.
It is a human readable text to identify the joining technology.
It is the classification of the joining process.
It is the list of entities associated to the joining process. Examples: ProductInstanceUri of the Controller.
This structure represents the data associated with Joining Result and the corresponding measurement values.
It provides the primary reason for failure. If a violation is handled by a rework step resulting in a successful operation, then it is not reported as failure.

It is an array of overall result values which contains the measurement values of different quantities like torque, angle, time, etc. based on the program.
Note: If the operation is terminated in the first step and no values are available to report, then this list may be empty.
It is an array of step results corresponding to each step in the program. This parameter is used if the system provides one or more step results.

It is an array of external errors which captures the failures outside the boundary of the program are captured as part of the Errors. It is up to the application to fill in the critical/primary error in the Errors list as top entry.
It is the identifier of the step result which is the primary cause of failure.
It is a structure to include the Joining Trace content. This can be NULL or empty and it is up to the application to send this as part of the Joining Result.

This structure is the base container for any joint component such as Bolt, Rivet, Gasket, Glue string, etc. Note: The concrete definition of joint component is not defined in this version of the specification.
It is the identifier of the joint component. Examples: BoltId, RivetId, GasketId, etc.
It is the name of the joint component.
It is the description of the joint component.
It is the manufacturer of the joint component.
It is the identifier of the manufacturer.
It is the content of the joint component. Examples: Bolt, Rivet, Gasket, etc.
This structure provides the joint information. Joint is the physical outcome of the joining operation which determines the properties of the point where multiple parts are assembled.
It is the identifier of the joint.

It is an identifier to track the changes made to the original instance in the system.
Example: It can point to the identifier of the object when it was created for the first time. It should be an existing ID in the system if it is available else it can be NULL if it is not tracked in a joining system.
It is the identifier of the associated joint design.
It is the time when the joint was created.
It is the time when the joint was updated last time. If it is not available, it can be same as CreationTime.
It is the name of the joint.
It is the description of the joint.
It is the classification of the joint.

It is the details of the classification. It can also be used to provide any other type of classification.
Examples: Safety Critical, etc.
It is the status of the joint. Examples: Ok, InUse, NotInUse, NotYetDone, etc.
It is the list of entities associated to the joint. Examples: JoiningProcessId, JobId, QualityProcessId, SimulationProcessId, etc.
It is a human readable text to identify the joining technology.
This structure provides the design information of a given joint.
It is the identifier of the joint design.
It is the name of the joint design.
It is the description of the joint design.
It is the list of design values.
It is the list of joint components associated to the joint design. Examples: BoltId, RivetId, GasketId, etc.
This structure is similar to 0:KeyValuePair which uses 0:TrimmedString instead of 0:QualifiedName.
It is the unique key of the value.
It is the value associated with the key.
This structure provides the given value and corresponding limits for a given physical quantity (if applicable).
It is the physical quantity of the value. Example: Torque, Angle, etc. Note: It may not be applicable for few reported values such as Software Version, etc.
It is the name of the given value.

It is the current value. The data type can be any simple data type such as Integer, String, Double, DateTime, etc. If the value is corresponding to the physical quantity, then the recommended data type is Double.

It is the previous value (if available). The data type can be any simple data type such as Integer, String, Double, DateTime, etc. If the value is corresponding to the physical quantity, then the data type should be Double.
It is the low limit of the given value. It is not applicable for values which do not have PhysicalQuantity.
It is the high limit of the given value. It is not applicable for values which do not have PhysicalQuantity.
It is the engineering unit of the CurrentValue, PreviousValue, LowLimit, HighLimit. It is not applicable for values which does not have PhysicalQuantity.
This structure is used to provide various types of counters associated to a Result. These counters are related to a joining process with sub-processes.
It is the name of the counter.
It is the value of the counter.
It is the type of the counter.
This structure is a subtype of ResultMetaDataType. It is used to define additional meta data of a Result in a joining system.

System-wide unique identifier, which is assigned by the system. This ID can be used for fetching exactly this result using the method GetResultById and it is identical to the ResultId of the ResultReadyEventType. If the system does not manage resultIds, it should always be set to “NA”.
Indicates that additional data for this result can be retrieved by temporary file transfer. If not provided, it is assumed that no file is available.
Indicates whether the result is the partial result of a total result. When not all samples are finished yet the result is 'partial'. If not provided, it is assumed to be a total result.

Indicates whether the result was created in simulation mode. Simulation mode implies that the result is only generated for testing purposes and not based on real production data. If not provided, it is assumed to not be simulated.

ResultState provides information about the current state of the process or measurement creating a result. Applications may use negative values for application-specific states. All other values shall only be used as defined in the following: 0 – Undefined initial value 1 – Completed: Processing was carried out completely 2 – Processing: Processing has not been finished yet 3 – Aborted: Processing was stopped at some point before completion 4 – Failed: Processing failed in some way

Identifies the step which produced the result. Although the system-wide unique JobId would be sufficient to identify the job which the result belongs to, this makes for easier filtering without keeping track of JobIds. This specification does not define how the stepId is transmitted to the system. Typically, it is provided by the client when starting an execution.

Identifies the part used to produce the result. Although the system-wide unique JobId would be sufficient to identify the job which the result belongs to, this makes for easier filtering without keeping track of JobIds. This specification does not define how the partId is transmitted to the system. Typically, it is provided by the client when starting the job.

External ID of the recipe in use which produced the result. The External ID is managed by the environment. This specification does not define how the externalRecipeId is transmitted to the system. Typically, it is provided by the client.
Internal ID of the recipe in use which produced the result. This ID is system-wide unique and it is assigned by the system.
Identifies the product used to produce the result. This specification does not define how the externalRecipeId is transmitted to the system. Typically, it is provided by the client.

External ID of the Configuration in use while the result was produced. It is managed by the Environment. This specification does not define how the externalConfigurationId is transmitted to the system. Typically, it is provided by the client.
Internal ID of the Configuration in use while the result was produced. This ID is system-wide unique and it is assigned by the system.
Identifies the job which produced the result. This ID is system-wide unique and it is assigned by the system.
CreationTime indicates the time when the result was created. Creation time on the measurement system (not the receive time of the server). It is recommended to always provide the creationTime.
Collection of different processing times that were needed to create the result.
Path to the actual measured result, managed external to the server.
The ResultEvaluation indicates whether the result was in tolerance.
Vendor-specific code describing more details on resultEvaluation.
The optional EvaluationDetails provides high level status information in a user-friendly text. This can be left empty for successful operations.

The format in which the measurement results are available (e.g. QDAS, CSV, …) using the ResultTransfer Object. If multiple file formats are provided, the GenerateFileForRead of ResultTransfer should contain corresponding transferOptions, to select the file format. This specification does not define those transferOptions.
It is a human readable text to identify the joining technology.
It is the cyclic counter which is incremented for each result generated.
It is the user-friendly name of the result. Examples: Job Result, Batch Result, etc.
It is the additional information associated with the result. It can contain information on the ResultContent.
It provides information on the classification of the result in the joining system.
It provides information on how the joining operation was performed.
It provides the type of joining operation.
It is a list of identifiers associated to the given result. Examples: ProductId, VIN, SocketId, JointId, JoiningProcessId, etc.
It is a list of counters associated to the given result. Examples: Batch Counter, Retry Counter, Channel Counter, etc.
It provides information on type of intervention which has occurred during the joining operation.

It indicates that the result is generated when the asset was offline. The default value is false. Note: The definition of offline status is application specific. Example: Wireless tool performing joining in radio shadow.
It is used to send any additional meta data which cannot be sent using the existing properties. It shall be used only for sending meta data but not any content.
It is used to report measurement values of the joining operation. Those are meant to characterize the quality of the process. It is used in JoiningResultDataType and StepResultDataType.
It is the measured value of the given result. The value corresponds to the PhysicalQuantity attribute of JoiningDataVariableType.
It is a user readable name of the given measurement value.
It indicates whether the measured value is as per the configured limits and corresponds to a successful result or not.
It is the identifier of the given value if it is available in the system.
It is an associated tag to the given measurement value to classify it based on the joining domain. Examples: FINAL, YIELD, SNUG, etc.
It is the index to the trace sample array from which corresponds to this specific result.

It is the time offset to point out the absolute time point in the array of trace samples. This may or may not match with an element in the TraceContent array. If it is not available in the TraceContent array, the value can be visualized in the trace graph via interpolation or some other plotting mechanisms.
It is an array of parameter identifiers configured in the Program which is being monitored or sampled. Note: In most common cases, it can be a single identifier.
Indicates whether the measured value is above or below the configured limit. It is only relevant if program or step configuration is violated.
It provides information on the consequence occurred due to the violation of the configurable limits. Examples: Step Transition, Abort Joining Operation. Is the consequence repairable or not, etc.
It is the system-wide unique identifier of the sensor which has reported the value.
It provides the lower limit of the measured value as per the program.
It provides the upper limit of the measured value as per the program.
It provides the target value of the specific measurement in the program step.

It provides the step number or name of the program step which has generated the result. Note: This is not applicable for StepResultValues in StepResultDataType. This is added for OverallResultValues in JoiningResultDataType to get the information of the step which has reported the specific value.
It is to determine the type of the physical quantity associated to a given value(s).
It is the engineering unit of the measured value.
This structure contains the signal information which is used in SetIOSignals and GetIOSignals methods.
It is the identifier of the signal.
It is the value of the signal.
It is the description of the signal.
It is the type of the signal.
This structure represents the measurement values corresponding to a given step in the program. It is used in JoiningResultDataType.
It is the system-wide unique identifier of the step result.
It is the system-wide unique identifier of the Program Step.
It is the step number or name of the program step. Sometimes the step id is like 2A, 3B based on number of branches. Hence a string place holder is needed instead of number.
It is the user-friendly name of the given step. This can be the name of the step in the configuration for ease of use and troubleshooting.
It indicates if the StepResult is successful or not.
It is the offset to the ProcessingTimes.StartTime in Result, this value can be used to order the steps in execution order if same step is run multiple times.
It is the system-wide unique identifier of the StepTrace associated to the StepResult.
It is the set of values which needs to be measured as per the program step.
It is to describe of the trace for a given program step. It is used in JoiningTraceDataType.
It is the system-wide unique identifier of the step trace.
It is the system-wide unique identifier of the associated step result.
It is the total number of trace points to be sent for each quantity.
It is the time between each sample in the given Trace array. This is required if applications want to reduce the amount of data by omitting the StepTraceContent array which represents TIME.
It is the offset to the ProcessingTimes.StartTime in the Result instance. This value can be used to order the steps in execution order if same step is run multiple times.

It is an array of trace data which contains the samples of different quantities like torque, angle, time, etc. The array size of TraceContent array should be same for each quantity and that is determined by the NumberOfTracePoints value.
It is to describe the trace samples for a given program step. It is used in StepTraceDataType.
It is an array of trace samples. The values correspond to the PhysicalQuantity attribute.
It is the system-wide unique identifier of the sensor which has reported the values. This will be useful for identifying the sensor when the same set of samples are reported by multiple sensors.
It is the user-friendly name for the given trace.
It is an additional text to describe the trace samples.
It is to determine the type of the physical quantity associated to a given value(s).
It is the engineering unit of the values.
It is a base type to encapsulate common data for a Trace.
It is the system-wide unique identifier of the Trace.
It is the system-wide unique identifier of the associated result. This is useful to link Result and Trace instances when the Result and Trace are sent separately.
This structure is to describe the content of traces for all the steps in the given program. It is used in JoiningResultDataType.
It is the system-wide unique identifier of the Trace.
It is the system-wide unique identifier of the associated result. This is useful to link Result and Trace instances when the Result and Trace are sent separately.
It is an array of StepTraceDataType which provides trace content for each step in the given result.

The JoiningDataVariableType is a subtype of the BaseDataVariableType. It is to describe common semantic required for variables in the system. In this version of the specification, it provides information about physical quantity and Engineering Units.
0:EngineeringUnits defines the engineering unit of the values.
PhysicalQuantity is to determine the type of the physical quantity associated to a given value(s).
The JoiningSystemEventContentType is a subtype of 0:BaseVariableType. It is used JoiningSystemEventType and JoiningSystemConditionType.
AssociatedEntities is a list of identifiers of various entities/objects available in the given system. Example: An event maybe associated to Asset, Result, Joint, Error, etc.
EventCode is a system specific event code associated to the given event.
EventText is a human readable text related to the context of the event.
JoiningTechnology is a human readable text to identify the joining technology which has triggered the event. Examples: Tightening, Gluing, Riveting, Flow Drill Fastening, etc.

ReportedValues is a list of values associated with the given event payload. Example: If it is an over temperature event, then the ReportedValue can be the measured value along with the corresponding limits.
The JoiningSystemResultType is a subtype of ResultType. It is used to expose the information of the ResultDataType in individual sub-variables.

System-wide unique identifier, which is assigned by the system. This ID can be used for fetching exactly this result using the method GetResultById and it is identical to the ResultId of the ResultReadyEventType. If the system does not manage resultIds, it should always be set to “NA”.
It provides the type of joining operation.
It is a list of identifiers associated to the given result. Examples: ProductId, VIN, SocketId, JointId, JoiningProcessId, etc.
It provides information on the classification of the result in the joining system.
It is the additional information associated with the result. It can contain information on the ResultContent.
It is used to send any additional meta data which cannot be sent using the existing properties. It shall be used only for sending meta data but not any content.
It provides information on type of intervention which has occurred during the joining operation.

It indicates that the result is generated when the asset was offline. The default value is false. Note: The definition of offline status is application specific. Example: Wireless tool performing joining in radio shadow.
It is a human readable text to identify the joining technology.
It is the user-friendly name of the result.
It provides information on how the joining operation was performed.
It is a list of counters associated to the given result. Examples: Batch Counter, Retry Counter, Channel Counter, etc.
It is a human readable text to identify the joining technology.
Indicates that an entity is accepted.
Indicates that an entity is added.
Indicates that the asset is connected.
Indicates that the asset is disabled.
Indicates that the asset is disconnected.
Indicates that the asset is enabled.
Indicates a change in the asset location.
Indicates a change in the certificate.
Indicates a change in the configuration.
Indicates a failure in the data validation.
The EntityExpiryWarningConditionClassType is used to classify events or conditions to indicate that an entity is about to expire within a joining system.
Indicates an error in the system.
Indicates that an entity is expired.
Indicates a change in the hardware entity.
Indicates that an entity is incompatible.
Indicates a failure in the input validation.
Indicates that an entity is invalid.
Indicates that a joining system user has logged-in.
Indicates that a joining system user logged-out.
Indicates a change in the license.
Indicates the change of the entity location from out-of-zone to in-zone.
Indicates the change in the entity location from in-zone to out-of-zone.
Indicates that an entity is missing.
Indicates that an entity is not available.
Indicates that an entity is not supported.
Indicates that an entity is received.
Indicates that an entity is rejected.
Indicates that an entity is removed.
Indicates that an entity is selected in a joining system.
Indicates that a given joining process is selected in a joining system.
Indicates a change in the software entity.
Indicates that an entity is started.
Indicates that an entity is stopped.
Indicates that a threshold is violated.
Indicates that a violated threshold is resolved.
Indicates the result memory in a joining system is above the configured limit of unacknowledged results.
Indicates that an entity is updated.

The JoiningSystemConditionType is used to send any type of events with acknowledgement mechanism from a joining system. Note: The type of event is determined by the usage of respective Condition Class(es) and Condition SubClass(es) properties defined in 0:ConditionType.
JoiningSystemEventContent is the common payload of the event from a joining system.
AssociatedEntities is a list of identifiers of various entities/objects available in the given system. Example: An event maybe associated to Asset, Result, Joint, Error, etc.
EventCode is a system specific event code associated to the given event.
EventText is a human readable text related to the context of the event.
JoiningTechnology is a human readable text to identify the joining technology which has triggered the event. Examples: Tightening, Gluing, Riveting, Flow Drill Fastening, etc.

ReportedValues is a list of values associated with the given event payload. Example: If it is an over temperature event, then the ReportedValue can be the measured value along with the corresponding limits.

The JoiningSystemEventType is used to send any type of events from a joining system. Note: The type of event is determined by the usage of respective Condition Class(es) and Condition SubClass(es) properties defined in 0:BaseEventType.
JoiningSystemEventContent is the common payload of the event from a joining system.
AssociatedEntities is a list of identifiers of various entities/objects available in the given system. Example: An event maybe associated to Asset, Result, Joint, Error, etc.
EventCode is a system specific event code associated to the given event.
EventText is a human readable text related to the context of the event.
JoiningTechnology is a human readable text to identify the joining technology which has triggered the event. Examples: Tightening, Gluing, Riveting, Flow Drill Fastening, etc.

ReportedValues is a list of values associated with the given event payload. Example: If it is an over temperature event, then the ReportedValue can be the measured value along with the corresponding limits.
This EventType provides information of a complete or partial result from a joining system.

System-wide unique identifier, which is assigned by the system. This ID can be used for fetching exactly this result using the method GetResultById and it is identical to the ResultId of the ResultReadyEventType. If the system does not manage resultIds, it should always be set to “NA”.
It provides the type of joining operation.
It is a list of identifiers associated to the given result. Examples: ProductId, VIN, SocketId, JointId, JoiningProcessId, etc.
It provides information on the classification of the result in the joining system.
It is the additional information associated with the result. It can contain information on the ResultContent.
It provides information on type of intervention which has occurred during the joining operation.

It indicates that the result is generated when the asset was offline. The default value is false. Note: The definition of offline status is application specific. Example: Wireless tool performing joining in radio shadow.
It is a human readable text to identify the joining technology.
It is the user-friendly name of the result.
It is a list of counters associated to the given result. Examples: Batch Counter, Retry Counter, Channel Counter, etc.
It is a human readable text to identify the joining technology.
This EventType provides the requested results from the Server using RequestResults method or RequestUnacknowledgedResults method.
The IJoiningAdditionalInformationType provides additional parameters for Identification of a given asset.
Description is the system specific description of the asset.
JoiningTechnology is a human readable text to identify the joining technology.
SupplierCode is the SAP or ERP Supplier Code of the asset.

This is a generic interface common for all assets in a given Joining System. The purpose of this interface is to provide a standard way of identification and common information for all the assets. This interface has a standard MachineryItemIdentificationType add-in which can be assigned with MachineIdentificationType or MachineryComponentIdentificationType for a given asset based on the requirement of the system.
The Health Object is an instance of FunctionalGroupType to group health related parameters for all the assets in a Joining System.
DeviceHealth indicates the status as defined by NAMUR Recommendation NE107. Clients can read or monitor this Variable to determine the device condition.
ErrorCode is the system specific code for the error occurred.
ErrorMessage is the user readable text of the error reported by the given asset.
ErrorTimestamp is the timestamp when the error occurred in the given asset.
Temperature is the measured temperature of the asset.

The Identification Object, using the standardized name defined in OPC 10000-100, provides identification information about the asset. This is a mandatory place holder and any asset inheriting IJoiningSystemAssetType will replace it with MachineIdentificationType or MachineryComponentIdentificationType.
A human-readable, localized name of the manufacturer of the MachineryItem.

A string containing a unique production number of the manufacturer of the MachineryItem. The global uniqueness of the serial number is only given in the context of the manufacturer, and potentially the model. The value shall not change during the life-cycle of the MachineryItem.
Description is the system specific description of the asset.
JoiningTechnology is a human readable text to identify the joining technology.
SupplierCode is the SAP or ERP Supplier Code of the asset.
It provides an entry point to various lifetime variables.
StartValue indicates the initial value, when there is still the full lifetime left.
LimitValue indicates when the end of lifetime has been reached.
The MachineryBuildingBlocks contains building blocks from OPC UA for Machinery.
It provides information about the duration something is turned on and how long it performs an activity.

OperationCycleCounter is counting the times the component switches from not performing an activity to performing an activity. For example, each time a valve starts moving, is counted. This value shall only increase during the lifetime of the component and shall not be reset when the component is restarted.

OperationDuration is the duration the MachineryItem has been powered and performing an activity. This counter is intended for machines and components where a distinction is made between switched on and in operation. For example, a drive might be powered on but not operating. It is not intended for machines or components always performing an activity like sensors always measuring data. This value shall only increase during the lifetime of the MachineryItem and shall not be reset when it is restarted. The OperationDuration is provided as Duration, i.e., in milliseconds or even fractions of a millisecond. However, the Server is not expected to update the value in such a high frequency, but maybe once a minute or once an hour, depending on the application.

PowerOnDuration is the duration the MachineryItem has been powered. The main purpose is to determine the time in which degradation of the MachineryItem occurred. The details, when the time is counted, is implementation-specific. Companion specifications might define specific rules. Typically, when the MachineryItem has supply voltage and the main CPU is running, the time is counted. This may include any kind of sleep mode, but may not include pure Wake on LAN. This value shall only increase during the lifetime of the MachineryItem and shall not be reset when it is restarted. The PowerOnDuration is provided as Duration, i.e., in milliseconds or even fractions of a millisecond. However, the Server is not expected to update the value in such a high frequency, but maybe once a minute or once an hour, depending on the application.
The Maintenance Object is an instance of FunctionalGroupType to group maintenance related parameters for the given asset in a Joining System.
The Calibration Object provides a set of parameters related to the calibration operations performed on a given asset.
CalibrationPlace is the location where the last calibration was completed.
CalibrationValue is the configured value of the calibration.

CertificateUri contains the URI of a certificate of the calibration target in case the calibration target is certified and the information available. Otherwise, the Variable should be omitted. The String shall be a URI as defined by RFC 3986. Example: MCE test document.
LastCalibration is the date when the last calibration was completed.
NextCalibration is the date of the next planned calibration.
SensorScale is the nominal scale of the sensor. It corresponds also with the measurement range of the sensor.
The Service Object provides a set of parameters related to the service operations performed on a given asset.
LastService is the date when the last service was completed.
NextService is the date of the next planned service.
NumberOfServices is the total number of services taken place.
RemainingCycles is the remaining cycles before the service or maintenance. It can go negative if a service is skipped to indicate overshoot cycles.
ServiceCycleCount is the total cycle counter since the last service.
ServiceCycleSpan is the maximum allowed number of cycles between two services.
ServiceOperationCycles is the value of the 2:OperationCycleCounter when the last service was performed.
ServicePlace is the location where the last service was completed.
ServiceReminderCycles is the configured threshold for the number of remaining cycles before the service reminder is sent. This is calculated based on the RemainingCycles.
ServiceReminderDays is the number of days before a service reminder should be sent.
Entry point for monitoring information of a MachineryItem.
Entry point for consumption information of the MachineryItem.
Entry point of health information of the MachineryItem.
DeviceHealth indicates the status as defined by NAMUR Recommendation NE107. Clients can read or monitor this Variable to determine the device condition.
ErrorCode is the system specific code for the error occurred.
ErrorMessage is the user readable text of the error reported by the given asset.
ErrorTimestamp is the timestamp when the error occurred in the given asset.
Temperature is the measured temperature of the asset.
Entry point for process information of the MachineryItem.

Entry point for status information of the MachineryItem. If this Object is provided, and the MachineryItemState is provided, it shall be referenced. If this Object is provided and the MachineryOperationMode is provided, it shall be referenced.
Provides notifications as events or objects.
The Parameters Object is an instance of 0:FolderType to group set of common parameters of an asset in a joining system.
Connected indicates if a given asset is connected or disconnected. It can change by DisconnectAsset method or by some other external interface.
Enabled indicates if a given asset is enabled or disabled. It can change by EnableAsset method or by some other external interface.
IOSignals is an array of signals available for the asset.
The Parameters Object is an instance of 0:FolderType to group set of common parameters of an asset in a joining system.
Type is a user readable open string to describe the type of accessory such as socket selector, operator panel, etc.
The Parameters Object is an instance of 0:FolderType to group set of common parameters of an asset in a joining system.
Capacity is the nominal capacity of the battery.
0:EngineeringUnits defines the engineering unit of the values.
PhysicalQuantity is to determine the type of the physical quantity associated to a given value(s).
ChargeCycleCount is the number of times the battery has been charged since the initial operation date.
NominalVoltage is the nominal DC voltage of the battery.
0:EngineeringUnits defines the engineering unit of the values.
PhysicalQuantity is to determine the type of the physical quantity associated to a given value(s).

StateOfCharge is the state of charge (SOC) indicator functions as a sort of fuel gauge that displays the usable amount of energy. This helps determine optimal charging and discharging. It is given in percentage.

StateOfHealth is the State of Health is a measurement that reflects the general condition of a battery and its ability to deliver the specified performance compared with a fresh battery. It considers such factors as charge acceptance, internal resistance, voltage, and self-discharge. It is given in percentage.
Type is a user readable text to determine the type of battery such as pack type, technology, chemical composition, battery standard, etc.
The Parameters Object is an instance of 0:FolderType to group set of common parameters of an asset in a joining system.
CableLength is the length of the cable.
Type is the classification of the cable.
The Parameters Object is an instance of 0:FolderType to group set of common parameters of an asset in a joining system.
Type is the classification of a Controller.
The Parameters Object is an instance of 0:FolderType to group set of common parameters of an asset in a joining system.
FeedingSpeed indicates the output in parts per time. Example: fasteners / second.
0:EngineeringUnits defines the engineering unit of the values.
PhysicalQuantity is to determine the type of the physical quantity associated to a given value(s).
FillLevel is the fill level in the feeder in percentage [%]. (0%=empty, 100% = full).
Material is the type or name of the part which is supplied by the feeder.
Type is the classification of a Feeder.
The Parameters Object is an instance of 0:FolderType to group set of common parameters of an asset in a joining system.
StorageCapacity is the static information on size of the storage in Bytes.
Type is the type of memory device. It may define the form factor, interface, or technology. Examples: Flash, CFAST, USB, etc.
UsedSpace is the static information on size of the used space in Bytes.
The Parameters Object is an instance of 0:FolderType to group set of common parameters of an asset in a joining system.
ActualPower is the actual load consumption of the power supply.
0:EngineeringUnits defines the engineering unit of the values.
PhysicalQuantity is to determine the type of the physical quantity associated to a given value(s).
InputSpecification is the input specification of the power supply. Example: 230 V, 50/60 Hz, 10 A.
NominalPower is the maximum output power of the power supply.
0:EngineeringUnits defines the engineering unit of the values.
PhysicalQuantity is to determine the type of the physical quantity associated to a given value(s).
OutputSpecification is the output specification of the power supply.
The Parameters Object is an instance of 0:FolderType to group set of common parameters of an asset in a joining system.
MeasuredValue is the actual measured value reported from a sensor.
OverloadCount is the number of overloads of the sensor, where the permissible load of the sensor was exceeded.
Type is the classification of a Sensor.
The Parameters Object is an instance of 0:FolderType to group set of common parameters of an asset in a joining system.
NodeNumber is the node identifier in multiple configurations. Examples: Cabinet with one controller and multiple servo/modules.
The Parameters Object is an instance of 0:FolderType to group set of common parameters of an asset in a joining system.
Type is a user readable open string to describe the type of subcomponent such as network module, etc.
The Parameters Object is an instance of 0:FolderType to group set of common parameters of an asset in a joining system.
Type is the classification of a Tool.
It provides identification parameters of the joining system.
The default BrowseName for instances of the type.
It is the description of the system which could be written by the customer to identify the system. It could be the purpose of the system in the assembly line.
IntegratorName is the name of the system integrator.
JoiningTechnology is a human readable text to identify the joining technology of the joining system.
Location is the location of the given system in the given plant or factory in text format.
Manufacturer provides a human-readable, localized name of the joining system manufacturer.
ManufacturerUri provides a unique identifier for this company. This identifier should be a fully qualified domain name; however, it may be a GUID or similar construct that ensures global uniqueness.
Model provides the type of the joining system. Examples: Fixtured System, Handheld System, etc.
Name is the name of the joining system. It can also be the standard browse name of the instance of JoiningSystemType.
ProductInstanceUri is a globally unique resource identifier provided by the manufacturer.
SystemId is the system integrator specific identifier for the system. It represents a reference to the manufacturer ERP system.
The JoiningProcessManagementType provides access to various joining processes in a joining system.
The Method AbortJoiningProcess is used to abort the input joining process if it is under execution.

With this argument the Client can provide the identifier of the asset on which this method is applicable. It can be empty if the method is modelled directly under the required asset. If it is empty, the system can consider the identifier of the asset where the Server is running.

It is the identification information of the joining process which can be used to select the joiningProcess.
If it includes joiningProcessId then it is used for the selection and other arguments are ignored.
If it does not include joiningProcessId, then the system checks for joiningProcessOriginId which will be used for the selection.
If joiningProcessId and joiningProcessOriginId are not available, then the system uses the selectionName for the selection of the joining process.
It is an optional message sent from the Client to the joining system to indicate the reason for aborting the joining operation.
It provides the status of the Method execution.
It provides the high-level status information in a user-friendly text.
The Method DecrementJoiningProcessCounter used to decrement the counter of the sequential joining processes such as Job, etc.

With this argument the Client can provide the identifier of the asset on which this method is applicable. It can be empty if the method is modelled directly under the required asset. If it is empty, the system can consider the identifier of the asset where the Server is running.

It is the identification information of the joining process which can be used to select the joiningProcess.
If it includes joiningProcessId then it is used for the selection and other arguments are ignored.
If it does not include joiningProcessId, then the system checks for joiningProcessOriginId which will be used for the selection.
If joiningProcessId and joiningProcessOriginId are not available, then the system uses the selectionName for the selection of the joining process.
It is the number of decrements to be done for the joining process counter. The default value is 1 if it is not provided.
It provides the status of the Method execution.
It provides the high-level status information in a user-friendly text.
The default BrowseName for instances of the type.
The Method DeleteJoiningProcess is used to delete the input joining process.

With this argument the Client can provide the identifier of the asset on which this method is applicable. It can be empty if the method is modelled directly under the required asset. If it is empty, the system can consider the identifier of the asset where the Server is running.

It is the identification information of the joining process which can be used to select the joiningProcess.
If it includes joiningProcessId then it is used for the selection and other arguments are ignored.
If it does not include joiningProcessId, then the system checks for joiningProcessOriginId which will be used for the selection.
If joiningProcessId and joiningProcessOriginId are not available, then the system uses the selectionName for the selection of the joining process.
It provides the status of the Method execution.
It provides the high-level status information in a user-friendly text.
The Method DeselectJoiningProcess is used to deselect any selected joining process.

With this argument the Client can provide the identifier of the asset on which this method is applicable. It can be empty if the method is modelled directly under the required asset. If it is empty, the system can consider the identifier of the asset where the Server is running.
It provides the status of the Method execution.
It provides the high-level status information in a user-friendly text.
The Method GetJoiningProcess is used to get the joining process based on the input identifier.

With this argument the Client can provide the identifier of the asset on which this method is applicable. It can be empty if the method is modelled directly under the required asset. If it is empty, the system can consider the identifier of the asset where the Server is running.
It is the identifier of the joining process.
It is the joining process available in the system.
It is the selection name of the joining process configured in the system.
It provides the status of the Method execution.
It provides the high-level status information in a user-friendly text.
The Method GetJoiningProcessList is used to get the list of joining process meta data available in the system.

With this argument the Client can provide the identifier of the asset on which this method is applicable. It can be empty if the method is modelled directly under the required asset. If it is empty, the system can consider the identifier of the asset where the Server is running.
It is the list of joining process meta data available in the system.
It provides the status of the Method execution.
It provides the high-level status information in a user-friendly text.
The Method GetJoiningProcessRevisionList is used to get the list available revisions of a specific joining process based on the joiningProcessOriginId.

With this argument the Client can provide the identifier of the asset on which this method is applicable. It can be empty if the method is modelled directly under the required asset. If it is empty, the system can consider the identifier of the asset where the Server is running.
It is the origin identifier of the joining process which is used to manage the revisions of a given joining process.
It is the list of joining process meta data available in the system.
It provides the status of the Method execution.
It provides the high-level status information in a user-friendly text.
The Method GetSelectedJoiningProgram is used to get the selected joining program for a given asset.

With this argument the Client can provide the identifier of the asset on which this method is applicable. It can be empty if the method is modelled directly under the required asset. If it is empty, the system can consider the identifier of the asset where the Server is running.
It is the selected joining program for the input asset.
It provides the status of the Method execution.
It provides the high-level status information in a user-friendly text.
The Method IncrementJoiningProcessCounter is used to increment the counter of the sequential joining processes such as Job, etc.

With this argument the Client can provide the identifier of the asset on which this method is applicable. It can be empty if the method is modelled directly under the required asset. If it is empty, the system can consider the identifier of the asset where the Server is running.

It is the identification information of the joining process which can be used to select the joiningProcess.
If it includes joiningProcessId then it is used for the selection and other arguments are ignored.
If it does not include joiningProcessId, then the system checks for joiningProcessOriginId which will be used for the selection.
If joiningProcessId and joiningProcessOriginId are not available, then the system uses the selectionName for the selection of the joining process.
It is the number of increments to be done for the joining process counter. The default value is 1 if it is not provided.
It provides the status of the Method execution.
It provides the high-level status information in a user-friendly text.
The Method ResetJoiningProcess is used to reset/restart the sequential joining processes such as Job, etc.

With this argument the Client can provide the identifier of the asset on which this method is applicable. It can be empty if the method is modelled directly under the required asset. If it is empty, the system can consider the identifier of the asset where the Server is running.

It is the identification information of the joining process which can be used to select the joiningProcess.
If it includes joiningProcessId then it is used for the selection and other arguments are ignored.
If it does not include joiningProcessId, then the system checks for joiningProcessOriginId which will be used for the selection.
If joiningProcessId and joiningProcessOriginId are not available, then the system uses the selectionName for the selection of the joining process.
It provides the status of the Method execution.
It provides the high-level status information in a user-friendly text.
The Method SelectJoiningProcess is used to select the joining process based on the input arguments.

With this argument the Client can provide the identifier of the asset on which this method is applicable. It can be empty if the method is modelled directly under the required asset. If it is empty, the system can consider the identifier of the asset where the Server is running.

It is the identification information of the joining process which can be used to select the joiningProcess.
If it includes joiningProcessId then it is used for the selection and other arguments are ignored.
If it does not include joiningProcessId, then the system checks for joiningProcessOriginId which will be used for the selection.
If joiningProcessId and joiningProcessOriginId are not available, then the system uses the selectionName for the selection of the joining process.
It provides the status of the Method execution.
It provides the high-level status information in a user-friendly text.

The Method SendJoiningProcess is used to send a joining process to the joining system. It can be used to insert a joining program or joining batch or joining job or any other process applicable to a joining system. It shall overwrite the joining process if it already exists in the joining system.

With this argument the Client can provide the identifier of the asset on which this method is applicable. It can be empty if the method is modelled directly under the required asset. If it is empty, the system can consider the identifier of the asset where the Server is running.
With this argument the Client can provide the content of the joining process.
With this argument the Client can provide the required selection name for the given joining process. It is optional and can be empty.
It provides the status of the Method execution.
It provides the high-level status information in a user-friendly text.
The Method SetJoiningProcessCounter is used to set the counter of a sequential joining processes (such as Job, etc.) to the given input value.

With this argument the Client can provide the identifier of the asset on which this method is applicable. It can be empty if the method is modelled directly under the required asset. If it is empty, the system can consider the identifier of the asset where the Server is running.

It is the identification information of the joining process which can be used to select the joiningProcess.
If it includes joiningProcessId then it is used for the selection and other arguments are ignored.
If it does not include joiningProcessId, then the system checks for joiningProcessOriginId which will be used for the selection.
If joiningProcessId and joiningProcessOriginId are not available, then the system uses the selectionName for the selection of the joining process.
It is the new counter value for the joining process.
It provides the status of the Method execution.
It provides the high-level status information in a user-friendly text.
The Method SetJoiningProcessMapping is used to set the mapping of the joining process in a joining system. It can be used to map a joining process to a selection name.

With this argument the Client can provide the identifier of the asset on which this method is applicable. It can be empty if the method is modelled directly under the required asset. If it is empty, the system can consider the identifier of the asset where the Server is running.

It is the identification information of the joining process which can be used to map the respective joiningProcessId with selectionName and joiningProcessOriginId.
It shall at least contain the joiningProcessId and selectionName.
It provides the status of the Method execution.
It provides the high-level status information in a user-friendly text.
The Method SetJoiningProcessSize is used to set the size of the batch joining process.

With this argument the Client can provide the identifier of the asset on which this method is applicable. It can be empty if the method is modelled directly under the required asset. If it is empty, the system can consider the identifier of the asset where the Server is running.

It is the identification information of the joining process which can be used to select the joiningProcess.
If it includes joiningProcessId then it is used for the selection and other arguments are ignored.
If it does not include joiningProcessId, then the system checks for joiningProcessOriginId which will be used for the selection.
If joiningProcessId and joiningProcessOriginId are not available, then the system uses the selectionName for the selection of the joining process.
It is the maximum counter size for the joining process.
It provides the status of the Method execution.
It provides the high-level status information in a user-friendly text.
The Method StartJoiningProcess is used to start the input joining process. Note: It is not intended to be used in a hard real-time use case.

With this argument the Client can provide the identifier of the asset on which this method is applicable. It can be empty if the method is modelled directly under the required asset. If it is empty, the system can consider the identifier of the asset where the Server is running.

It is the identification information of the joining process which can be used to select the joiningProcess.
If it includes joiningProcessId then it is used for the selection and other arguments are ignored.
If it does not include joiningProcessId, then the system checks for joiningProcessOriginId which will be used for the selection.
If joiningProcessId and joiningProcessOriginId are not available, then the system uses the selectionName for the selection of the joining process.
It is the list of identifiers used for performing the joining operation. It is optional and can be empty.
It provides the status of the Method execution.
It provides the high-level status information in a user-friendly text.

The Method StartSelectedJoining is used to start the selected joining. The joining operation can be selected using SelectJoiningProcess or SelectJoint. Note: It is not intended to be used in a hard real-time use case.

With this argument the Client can provide the identifier of the asset on which this method is applicable. It can be empty if the method is modelled directly under the required asset. If it is empty, the system can consider the identifier of the asset where the Server is running.
If True, it will deselect the existing joining process after the joining operation is completed. The default value is False.
It provides the status of the Method execution.
It provides the high-level status information in a user-friendly text.
The JoiningSystemAssetMethodSetType provides a set of methods for various assets in a joining system.
The default BrowseName for instances of the type.
The Method DisconnectAsset is used to disconnect or connect the asset.

With this argument the Client can provide the identifier of the asset on which this method is applicable. It can be empty if the method is modelled directly under the required asset. If it is empty, the system can consider the identifier of the asset where the Server is running.
If true, it will prepare the asset for disconnect. The default value is false.
It provides the status of the Method execution.
It provides the high-level status information in a user-friendly text.
The Method EnableAsset is used to Enable or Disable a given asset. It is mostly applicable for Tool. The joining system can report a respective event when an asset is enabled or disabled.

With this argument the Client can provide the identifier of the asset on which this method is applicable. It can be empty if the method is modelled directly under the required asset. If it is empty, the system can consider the identifier of the asset where the Server is running.

If true, it will enable the asset, else it will disable the asset. The default value is false. Note: If the asset is performing the joining operation when the method is executed, then it shall disable the asset after the current operation.
It provides the status of the Method execution.
It provides the high-level status information in a user-friendly text.
The Method ExecuteOperation is an application specific interface to execute any generic operations supported by a joining system.

With this argument the Client can provide the identifier of the asset on which this method is applicable. It can be empty if the method is modelled directly under the required asset. If it is empty, the system can consider the identifier of the asset where the Server is running.
It is the type of operation. The list of integer values corresponding to a specific operation is provided by the documentation or the joining system via some interface.
It is the optional text to provide information on the type of operation.
It is the optional vendor’s name provided to identify the type of operations supported.
It provides the status of the Method execution.
It provides the high-level status information in a user-friendly text.
The Method GetErrorInformation is used to get the error information based on the input identifier. The details returned from the joining system is application specific.

With this argument the Client can provide the identifier of the asset on which this method is applicable. It can be empty if the method is modelled directly under the required asset. If it is empty, the system can consider the identifier of the asset where the Server is running.
It is the identifier of the error. It could be available as part of the JoiningResult. Note: If it is empty, the Server is allowed to return the latest error available.
It is the detailed error information. Examples: Log file, Detailed Error Information or Event Logs, etc.
It provides the status of the Method execution.
It provides the high-level status information in a user-friendly text.
The Method GetFeedbackFileList is used to get the list of feedback files from the asset.

With this argument the Client can provide the identifier of the asset on which this method is applicable. It can be empty if the method is modelled directly under the required asset. If it is empty, the system can consider the identifier of the asset where the Server is running.
It is the list of feedback files available in the system. It contains the feedback filenames or the file paths which can be used as an input in SendFeedback method.
It provides the status of the Method execution.
It provides the high-level status information in a user-friendly text.
The Method GetIdentifiers is used to get the list of identifiers available in the system which were managed by external systems.

With this argument the Client can provide the identifier of the asset on which this method is applicable. It can be empty if the method is modelled directly under the required asset. If it is empty, the system can consider the identifier of the asset where the Server is running.
The list of names of the identifiers which are requested. If it is empty, then all available identifiers are returned.
It is the list of identifiers available in the joining system based on the input criteria.
It provides the status of the Method execution.
It provides the high-level status information in a user-friendly text.
The Method GetIOSignals is used to get the list of available signals from the asset.

With this argument the Client can provide the identifier of the asset on which this method is applicable. It can be empty if the method is modelled directly under the required asset. If it is empty, the system can consider the identifier of the asset where the Server is running.
It is the list of signal identifiers requested. If it is empty, then all the available signals are returned from the asset.
It is the list of signals which are available in the asset based on the input signalIdList. If the signalIdList is empty, then all the available signals are sent from the asset.
It provides the status of the Method execution.
It provides the high-level status information in a user-friendly text.
The Method RebootAsset is used to reboot an asset.

With this argument the Client can provide the identifier of the asset on which this method is applicable. It can be empty if the method is modelled directly under the required asset. If it is empty, the system can consider the identifier of the asset where the Server is running.
It provides the status of the Method execution.
It provides the high-level status information in a user-friendly text.
The Method ResetIdentifiers is used to reset the specified identifiers.

With this argument the Client can provide the identifier of the asset on which this method is applicable. It can be empty if the method is modelled directly under the required asset. If it is empty, the system can consider the identifier of the asset where the Server is running.

It is the list of names of the identifiers which are requested to be reset. If it is NOT empty, then resetAll and resetLatest flags are ignored. If it is empty, then the resetAll or resetLatest flag is used.
If True, it will reset all the identifiers available in the joining system and resetLatest flag is ignored. If False and identifierList is empty then the resetLatest flag is used.

If True, it will reset the latest identifier available in the system. Note: This is provided for supporting legacy systems. The criteria to determine which identifier is latest is application specific.
It provides the status of the Method execution.
It provides the high-level status information in a user-friendly text.
The Method SendFeedback is used to send any type of feedback to a given asset. The feedback can be a text input or other types of feedback supported by the asset.

With this argument the Client can provide the identifier of the asset on which this method is applicable. It can be empty if the method is modelled directly under the required asset. If it is empty, the system can consider the identifier of the asset where the Server is running.
It is the type of feedback and has the following pre-defined values: 0 – UNDEFINED 1 – OTHER 2 – TEXT 3 – VISUAL 4 – AUDIO 5 – VIBRATE
It is the text feedback if the feedbackType is TEXT.

It is the file available in the asset which needs to be run for different types of feedback such as AUDIO, VIBRATE, etc. This can be retrieved using the GetFeedbackFileList method. It is optional and not applicable for text feedback.
It provides the status of the Method execution.
It provides the high-level status information in a user-friendly text.

The Method SendIdentifiers is used to send one or more identifiers to the joining system. These identifiers can be used for selection of a joining process, etc. These identifiers can often be part of the generated result. The input argument to this method is an array of EntityDataType structure where every entity in the joining system can be associated to a specific type for filtering.

With this argument the Client can provide the identifier of the asset on which this method is applicable. It can be empty if the method is modelled directly under the required asset. If it is empty, the system can consider the identifier of the asset where the Server is running.
It is the list of identifiers sent to the joining system.
It provides the status of the Method execution.
It provides the high-level status information in a user-friendly text.

The Method SendTextIdentifiers is used to send one or more identifiers to a joining system. These identifiers can be used for selection of a joining process, etc. These identifiers can often be part of the generated result. Note: The decision on which set of identifiers are used for the selection of a joining process and which set of identifiers should be part of the generated result is application specific.

With this argument the Client can provide the identifier of the asset on which this method is applicable. It can be empty if the method is modelled directly under the required asset. If it is empty, the system can consider the identifier of the asset where the Server is running.
It is the list of identifiers sent to the joining system.
It provides the status of the Method execution.
It provides the high-level status information in a user-friendly text.
The Method SetCalibration is used to set the calibration information of a given asset. It is intended to set the basic calibration information and does not cover the certification process.

With this argument the Client can provide the identifier of the asset on which this method is applicable. It can be empty if the method is modelled directly under the required asset. If it is empty, the system can consider the identifier of the asset where the Server is running.
It is the input calibration data which needs to be configured for the asset.
It provides the status of the Method execution.
It provides the high-level status information in a user-friendly text.
The Method SetIOSignals is used to set a list of IO signals of the asset. The type of operations mapped to each signal is application specific.

With this argument the Client can provide the identifier of the asset on which this method is applicable. It can be empty if the method is modelled directly under the required asset. If it is empty, the system can consider the identifier of the asset where the Server is running.
It is the list of signals which needs to be set in the asset.
It is the list of status for each signal. 0 – OK Values > 0 are reserved for errors defined by this and future standards. Values < 0 shall be used for application-specific errors.
It provides the status of the Method execution.
It provides the high-level status information in a user-friendly text.

The Method SetOfflineTimer is used to set the offline timer for the asset to determine how long the asset can perform the joining operations in an offline mode. Note: If an asset performs the joining operation in offline mode after setting the offline timer, the corresponding results generated shall have the IsGeneratedOffline flag set to TRUE.

With this argument the Client can provide the identifier of the asset on which this method is applicable. It can be empty if the method is modelled directly under the required asset. If it is empty, the system can consider the identifier of the asset where the Server is running.
It is the offlineTimer to be set. The behaviour of the asset when the timer is elapsed is application specific.
It provides the status of the Method execution.
It provides the high-level status information in a user-friendly text.

The Method SetTime is used to set the time of the asset manually. It is recommended to be used only when an asset does not have automated time synchronization. The joining system can report a respective event when the time is configured manually using this method.

With this argument the Client can provide the identifier of the asset on which this method is applicable. It can be empty if the method is modelled directly under the required asset. If it is empty, the system can consider the identifier of the asset where the Server is running.
It is the input time to be configured in the asset.
It provides the status of the Method execution.
It provides the high-level status information in a user-friendly text.
The JoiningSystemType provides the overview of the information exposed from a given joining system.
The AssetManagement Object is an instance of FunctionalGroupType to group assets and related objects in the joining system.
The Assets Object is an instance of FolderType to group set of assets available in the given system.
The MethodSet Object is an instance of JoiningSystemAssetMethodSetType which provides set of methods for various assets in a joining system.
The Method DisconnectAsset is used to disconnect or connect the asset.

With this argument the Client can provide the identifier of the asset on which this method is applicable. It can be empty if the method is modelled directly under the required asset. If it is empty, the system can consider the identifier of the asset where the Server is running.
If true, it will prepare the asset for disconnect. The default value is false.
It provides the status of the Method execution.
It provides the high-level status information in a user-friendly text.
The Method EnableAsset is used to Enable or Disable a given asset. It is mostly applicable for Tool. The joining system can report a respective event when an asset is enabled or disabled.

With this argument the Client can provide the identifier of the asset on which this method is applicable. It can be empty if the method is modelled directly under the required asset. If it is empty, the system can consider the identifier of the asset where the Server is running.

If true, it will enable the asset, else it will disable the asset. The default value is false. Note: If the asset is performing the joining operation when the method is executed, then it shall disable the asset after the current operation.
It provides the status of the Method execution.
It provides the high-level status information in a user-friendly text.
The Method ExecuteOperation is an application specific interface to execute any generic operations supported by a joining system.

With this argument the Client can provide the identifier of the asset on which this method is applicable. It can be empty if the method is modelled directly under the required asset. If it is empty, the system can consider the identifier of the asset where the Server is running.
It is the type of operation. The list of integer values corresponding to a specific operation is provided by the documentation or the joining system via some interface.
It is the optional text to provide information on the type of operation.
It is the optional vendor’s name provided to identify the type of operations supported.
It provides the status of the Method execution.
It provides the high-level status information in a user-friendly text.
The Method GetErrorInformation is used to get the error information based on the input identifier. The details returned from the joining system is application specific.

With this argument the Client can provide the identifier of the asset on which this method is applicable. It can be empty if the method is modelled directly under the required asset. If it is empty, the system can consider the identifier of the asset where the Server is running.
It is the identifier of the error. It could be available as part of the JoiningResult. Note: If it is empty, the Server is allowed to return the latest error available.
It is the detailed error information. Examples: Log file, Detailed Error Information or Event Logs, etc.
It provides the status of the Method execution.
It provides the high-level status information in a user-friendly text.
The Method GetFeedbackFileList is used to get the list of feedback files from the asset.

With this argument the Client can provide the identifier of the asset on which this method is applicable. It can be empty if the method is modelled directly under the required asset. If it is empty, the system can consider the identifier of the asset where the Server is running.
It is the list of feedback files available in the system. It contains the feedback filenames or the file paths which can be used as an input in SendFeedback method.
It provides the status of the Method execution.
It provides the high-level status information in a user-friendly text.
The Method GetIdentifiers is used to get the list of identifiers available in the system which were managed by external systems.

With this argument the Client can provide the identifier of the asset on which this method is applicable. It can be empty if the method is modelled directly under the required asset. If it is empty, the system can consider the identifier of the asset where the Server is running.
The list of names of the identifiers which are requested. If it is empty, then all available identifiers are returned.
It is the list of identifiers available in the joining system based on the input criteria.
It provides the status of the Method execution.
It provides the high-level status information in a user-friendly text.
The Method GetIOSignals is used to get the list of available signals from the asset.

With this argument the Client can provide the identifier of the asset on which this method is applicable. It can be empty if the method is modelled directly under the required asset. If it is empty, the system can consider the identifier of the asset where the Server is running.
It is the list of signal identifiers requested. If it is empty, then all the available signals are returned from the asset.
It is the list of signals which are available in the asset based on the input signalIdList. If the signalIdList is empty, then all the available signals are sent from the asset.
It provides the status of the Method execution.
It provides the high-level status information in a user-friendly text.
The Method RebootAsset is used to reboot an asset.

With this argument the Client can provide the identifier of the asset on which this method is applicable. It can be empty if the method is modelled directly under the required asset. If it is empty, the system can consider the identifier of the asset where the Server is running.
It provides the status of the Method execution.
It provides the high-level status information in a user-friendly text.
The Method ResetIdentifiers is used to reset the specified identifiers.

With this argument the Client can provide the identifier of the asset on which this method is applicable. It can be empty if the method is modelled directly under the required asset. If it is empty, the system can consider the identifier of the asset where the Server is running.

It is the list of names of the identifiers which are requested to be reset. If it is NOT empty, then resetAll and resetLatest flags are ignored. If it is empty, then the resetAll or resetLatest flag is used.
If True, it will reset all the identifiers available in the joining system and resetLatest flag is ignored. If False and identifierList is empty then the resetLatest flag is used.

If True, it will reset the latest identifier available in the system. Note: This is provided for supporting legacy systems. The criteria to determine which identifier is latest is application specific.
It provides the status of the Method execution.
It provides the high-level status information in a user-friendly text.
The Method SendFeedback is used to send any type of feedback to a given asset. The feedback can be a text input or other types of feedback supported by the asset.

With this argument the Client can provide the identifier of the asset on which this method is applicable. It can be empty if the method is modelled directly under the required asset. If it is empty, the system can consider the identifier of the asset where the Server is running.
It is the type of feedback and has the following pre-defined values: 0 – UNDEFINED 1 – OTHER 2 – TEXT 3 – VISUAL 4 – AUDIO 5 – VIBRATE
It is the text feedback if the feedbackType is TEXT.

It is the file available in the asset which needs to be run for different types of feedback such as AUDIO, VIBRATE, etc. This can be retrieved using the GetFeedbackFileList method. It is optional and not applicable for text feedback.
It provides the status of the Method execution.
It provides the high-level status information in a user-friendly text.

The Method SendIdentifiers is used to send one or more identifiers to the joining system. These identifiers can be used for selection of a joining process, etc. These identifiers can often be part of the generated result. The input argument to this method is an array of EntityDataType structure where every entity in the joining system can be associated to a specific type for filtering.

With this argument the Client can provide the identifier of the asset on which this method is applicable. It can be empty if the method is modelled directly under the required asset. If it is empty, the system can consider the identifier of the asset where the Server is running.
It is the list of identifiers sent to the joining system.
It provides the status of the Method execution.
It provides the high-level status information in a user-friendly text.
