The Method SendTextIdentifiers is used to send one or more identifiers to a joining system. These identifiers can be used for selection of a joining process, etc. These identifiers can often be part of the generated result. Note: The decision on which set of identifiers are used for the selection of a joining process and which set of identifiers should be part of the generated result is application specific.

With this argument the Client can provide the identifier of the asset on which this method is applicable. It can be empty if the method is modelled directly under the required asset. If it is empty, the system can consider the identifier of the asset where the Server is running.
It is the list of identifiers sent to the joining system.
It provides the status of the Method execution.
It provides the high-level status information in a user-friendly text.
The Method SetCalibration is used to set the calibration information of a given asset. It is intended to set the basic calibration information and does not cover the certification process.

With this argument the Client can provide the identifier of the asset on which this method is applicable. It can be empty if the method is modelled directly under the required asset. If it is empty, the system can consider the identifier of the asset where the Server is running.
It is the input calibration data which needs to be configured for the asset.
It provides the status of the Method execution.
It provides the high-level status information in a user-friendly text.
The Method SetIOSignals is used to set a list of IO signals of the asset. The type of operations mapped to each signal is application specific.

With this argument the Client can provide the identifier of the asset on which this method is applicable. It can be empty if the method is modelled directly under the required asset. If it is empty, the system can consider the identifier of the asset where the Server is running.
It is the list of signals which needs to be set in the asset.
It is the list of status for each signal. 0 – OK Values > 0 are reserved for errors defined by this and future standards. Values < 0 shall be used for application-specific errors.
It provides the status of the Method execution.
It provides the high-level status information in a user-friendly text.

The Method SetOfflineTimer is used to set the offline timer for the asset to determine how long the asset can perform the joining operations in an offline mode. Note: If an asset performs the joining operation in offline mode after setting the offline timer, the corresponding results generated shall have the IsGeneratedOffline flag set to TRUE.

With this argument the Client can provide the identifier of the asset on which this method is applicable. It can be empty if the method is modelled directly under the required asset. If it is empty, the system can consider the identifier of the asset where the Server is running.
It is the offlineTimer to be set. The behaviour of the asset when the timer is elapsed is application specific.
It provides the status of the Method execution.
It provides the high-level status information in a user-friendly text.

The Method SetTime is used to set the time of the asset manually. It is recommended to be used only when an asset does not have automated time synchronization. The joining system can report a respective event when the time is configured manually using this method.

With this argument the Client can provide the identifier of the asset on which this method is applicable. It can be empty if the method is modelled directly under the required asset. If it is empty, the system can consider the identifier of the asset where the Server is running.
It is the input time to be configured in the asset.
It provides the status of the Method execution.
It provides the high-level status information in a user-friendly text.
The Identification Object provides identification parameters of the joining system.
It is the description of the system which could be written by the customer to identify the system. It could be the purpose of the system in the assembly line.
IntegratorName is the name of the system integrator.
JoiningTechnology is a human readable text to identify the joining technology of the joining system.
Location is the location of the given system in the given plant or factory in text format.
Manufacturer provides a human-readable, localized name of the joining system manufacturer.
ManufacturerUri provides a unique identifier for this company. This identifier should be a fully qualified domain name; however, it may be a GUID or similar construct that ensures global uniqueness.
Model provides the type of the joining system. Examples: Fixtured System, Handheld System, etc.
Name is the name of the joining system. It can also be the standard browse name of the instance of JoiningSystemType.
ProductInstanceUri is a globally unique resource identifier provided by the manufacturer.
SystemId is the system integrator specific identifier for the system. It represents a reference to the manufacturer ERP system.
The JoiningProcessManagement Object is an instance of JoiningProcessManagementType which provides mechanisms to manage joining processes in the joining system.
The Method AbortJoiningProcess is used to abort the input joining process if it is under execution.

With this argument the Client can provide the identifier of the asset on which this method is applicable. It can be empty if the method is modelled directly under the required asset. If it is empty, the system can consider the identifier of the asset where the Server is running.

It is the identification information of the joining process which can be used to select the joiningProcess.
If it includes joiningProcessId then it is used for the selection and other arguments are ignored.
If it does not include joiningProcessId, then the system checks for joiningProcessOriginId which will be used for the selection.
If joiningProcessId and joiningProcessOriginId are not available, then the system uses the selectionName for the selection of the joining process.
It is an optional message sent from the Client to the joining system to indicate the reason for aborting the joining operation.
It provides the status of the Method execution.
It provides the high-level status information in a user-friendly text.
The Method DecrementJoiningProcessCounter used to decrement the counter of the sequential joining processes such as Job, etc.

With this argument the Client can provide the identifier of the asset on which this method is applicable. It can be empty if the method is modelled directly under the required asset. If it is empty, the system can consider the identifier of the asset where the Server is running.

It is the identification information of the joining process which can be used to select the joiningProcess.
If it includes joiningProcessId then it is used for the selection and other arguments are ignored.
If it does not include joiningProcessId, then the system checks for joiningProcessOriginId which will be used for the selection.
If joiningProcessId and joiningProcessOriginId are not available, then the system uses the selectionName for the selection of the joining process.
It is the number of decrements to be done for the joining process counter. The default value is 1 if it is not provided.
It provides the status of the Method execution.
It provides the high-level status information in a user-friendly text.
The Method DeselectJoiningProcess is used to deselect any selected joining process.

With this argument the Client can provide the identifier of the asset on which this method is applicable. It can be empty if the method is modelled directly under the required asset. If it is empty, the system can consider the identifier of the asset where the Server is running.
It provides the status of the Method execution.
It provides the high-level status information in a user-friendly text.
The Method GetJoiningProcessList is used to get the list of joining process meta data available in the system.

With this argument the Client can provide the identifier of the asset on which this method is applicable. It can be empty if the method is modelled directly under the required asset. If it is empty, the system can consider the identifier of the asset where the Server is running.
It is the list of joining process meta data available in the system.
It provides the status of the Method execution.
It provides the high-level status information in a user-friendly text.
The Method GetJoiningProcessRevisionList is used to get the list available revisions of a specific joining process based on the joiningProcessOriginId.

With this argument the Client can provide the identifier of the asset on which this method is applicable. It can be empty if the method is modelled directly under the required asset. If it is empty, the system can consider the identifier of the asset where the Server is running.
It is the origin identifier of the joining process which is used to manage the revisions of a given joining process.
It is the list of joining process meta data available in the system.
It provides the status of the Method execution.
It provides the high-level status information in a user-friendly text.
The Method IncrementJoiningProcessCounter is used to increment the counter of the sequential joining processes such as Job, etc.

With this argument the Client can provide the identifier of the asset on which this method is applicable. It can be empty if the method is modelled directly under the required asset. If it is empty, the system can consider the identifier of the asset where the Server is running.

It is the identification information of the joining process which can be used to select the joiningProcess.
If it includes joiningProcessId then it is used for the selection and other arguments are ignored.
If it does not include joiningProcessId, then the system checks for joiningProcessOriginId which will be used for the selection.
If joiningProcessId and joiningProcessOriginId are not available, then the system uses the selectionName for the selection of the joining process.
It is the number of increments to be done for the joining process counter. The default value is 1 if it is not provided.
It provides the status of the Method execution.
It provides the high-level status information in a user-friendly text.
The Method ResetJoiningProcess is used to reset/restart the sequential joining processes such as Job, etc.

With this argument the Client can provide the identifier of the asset on which this method is applicable. It can be empty if the method is modelled directly under the required asset. If it is empty, the system can consider the identifier of the asset where the Server is running.

It is the identification information of the joining process which can be used to select the joiningProcess.
If it includes joiningProcessId then it is used for the selection and other arguments are ignored.
If it does not include joiningProcessId, then the system checks for joiningProcessOriginId which will be used for the selection.
If joiningProcessId and joiningProcessOriginId are not available, then the system uses the selectionName for the selection of the joining process.
It provides the status of the Method execution.
It provides the high-level status information in a user-friendly text.
The Method SelectJoiningProcess is used to select the joining process based on the input arguments.

With this argument the Client can provide the identifier of the asset on which this method is applicable. It can be empty if the method is modelled directly under the required asset. If it is empty, the system can consider the identifier of the asset where the Server is running.

It is the identification information of the joining process which can be used to select the joiningProcess.
If it includes joiningProcessId then it is used for the selection and other arguments are ignored.
If it does not include joiningProcessId, then the system checks for joiningProcessOriginId which will be used for the selection.
If joiningProcessId and joiningProcessOriginId are not available, then the system uses the selectionName for the selection of the joining process.
It provides the status of the Method execution.
It provides the high-level status information in a user-friendly text.

The Method SendJoiningProcess is used to send a joining process to the joining system. It can be used to insert a joining program or joining batch or joining job or any other process applicable to a joining system. It shall overwrite the joining process if it already exists in the joining system.

With this argument the Client can provide the identifier of the asset on which this method is applicable. It can be empty if the method is modelled directly under the required asset. If it is empty, the system can consider the identifier of the asset where the Server is running.
With this argument the Client can provide the content of the joining process.
With this argument the Client can provide the required selection name for the given joining process. It is optional and can be empty.
It provides the status of the Method execution.
It provides the high-level status information in a user-friendly text.
The Method SetJoiningProcessCounter is used to set the counter of a sequential joining processes (such as Job, etc.) to the given input value.

With this argument the Client can provide the identifier of the asset on which this method is applicable. It can be empty if the method is modelled directly under the required asset. If it is empty, the system can consider the identifier of the asset where the Server is running.

It is the identification information of the joining process which can be used to select the joiningProcess.
If it includes joiningProcessId then it is used for the selection and other arguments are ignored.
If it does not include joiningProcessId, then the system checks for joiningProcessOriginId which will be used for the selection.
If joiningProcessId and joiningProcessOriginId are not available, then the system uses the selectionName for the selection of the joining process.
It is the new counter value for the joining process.
It provides the status of the Method execution.
It provides the high-level status information in a user-friendly text.
The Method SetJoiningProcessMapping is used to set the mapping of the joining process in a joining system. It can be used to map a joining process to a selection name.

With this argument the Client can provide the identifier of the asset on which this method is applicable. It can be empty if the method is modelled directly under the required asset. If it is empty, the system can consider the identifier of the asset where the Server is running.

It is the identification information of the joining process which can be used to map the respective joiningProcessId with selectionName and joiningProcessOriginId.
It shall at least contain the joiningProcessId and selectionName.
It provides the status of the Method execution.
It provides the high-level status information in a user-friendly text.
The Method SetJoiningProcessSize is used to set the size of the batch joining process.

With this argument the Client can provide the identifier of the asset on which this method is applicable. It can be empty if the method is modelled directly under the required asset. If it is empty, the system can consider the identifier of the asset where the Server is running.

It is the identification information of the joining process which can be used to select the joiningProcess.
If it includes joiningProcessId then it is used for the selection and other arguments are ignored.
If it does not include joiningProcessId, then the system checks for joiningProcessOriginId which will be used for the selection.
If joiningProcessId and joiningProcessOriginId are not available, then the system uses the selectionName for the selection of the joining process.
It is the maximum counter size for the joining process.
It provides the status of the Method execution.
It provides the high-level status information in a user-friendly text.
The Method StartJoiningProcess is used to start the input joining process. Note: It is not intended to be used in a hard real-time use case.

With this argument the Client can provide the identifier of the asset on which this method is applicable. It can be empty if the method is modelled directly under the required asset. If it is empty, the system can consider the identifier of the asset where the Server is running.

It is the identification information of the joining process which can be used to select the joiningProcess.
If it includes joiningProcessId then it is used for the selection and other arguments are ignored.
If it does not include joiningProcessId, then the system checks for joiningProcessOriginId which will be used for the selection.
If joiningProcessId and joiningProcessOriginId are not available, then the system uses the selectionName for the selection of the joining process.
It is the list of identifiers used for performing the joining operation. It is optional and can be empty.
It provides the status of the Method execution.
It provides the high-level status information in a user-friendly text.
The JointManagement Object is an instance of JointManagementType which provides mechanisms to manage joint and associated information.
The Method GetJoint is used to get the joint based on the input identifier.

With this argument the Client can provide the identifier of the asset on which this method is applicable. It can be empty if the method is modelled directly under the required asset. If it is empty, the system can consider the identifier of the asset where the Server is running.
It is the identifier of the joint.
It is the joint based on the input identifier.
It provides the status of the Method execution.
It provides the high-level status information in a user-friendly text.
The Method GetJointComponent is used to get the joint component based on the input identifier.

With this argument the Client can provide the identifier of the asset on which this method is applicable. It can be empty if the method is modelled directly under the required asset. If it is empty, the system can consider the identifier of the asset where the Server is running.
It is the identifier of the joint component.
It is the joint component based on the input identifier.

It provides the high-level status information in a user-friendly text.
The Method GetJointComponentList is used to get the list of available joint components in the system.

With this argument the Client can provide the identifier of the asset on which this method is applicable. It can be empty if the method is modelled directly under the required asset. If it is empty, the system can consider the identifier of the asset where the Server is running.
It is the list of joint components available in the system.
It provides the status of the Method execution.
It provides the high-level status information in a user-friendly text.
The Method GetJointDesign is used to get the joint design based on the input identifier.

With this argument the Client can provide the identifier of the asset on which this method is applicable. It can be empty if the method is modelled directly under the required asset. If it is empty, the system can consider the identifier of the asset where the Server is running.
It is the joint design based on the input identifier.
It is the joint design based on the input identifier.
It provides the status of the Method execution.
It provides the high-level status information in a user-friendly text.
The Method GetJointDesignList is used to get the list of available joint designs in the system.

With this argument the Client can provide the identifier of the asset on which this method is applicable. It can be empty if the method is modelled directly under the required asset. If it is empty, the system can consider the identifier of the asset where the Server is running.
It is the list of joint designs available in the system.
It provides the status of the Method execution.
It provides the high-level status information in a user-friendly text.
The Method GetJointList is used to get the list of available joints in the system.

With this argument the Client can provide the identifier of the asset on which this method is applicable. It can be empty if the method is modelled directly under the required asset. If it is empty, the system can consider the identifier of the asset where the Server is running.
It is the list of joints available in the system.
It provides the status of the Method execution.
It provides the high-level status information in a user-friendly text.
The Method GetJointRevisionList is used to get the list available revisions of a specific joint based on the JointOriginId.

With this argument the Client can provide the identifier of the asset on which this method is applicable. It can be empty if the method is modelled directly under the required asset. If it is empty, the system can consider the identifier of the asset where the Server is running.
It is the origin identifier of the joint which is used to manage the revisions of a given joint.
It is the list of joints available in the system.
It provides the status of the Method execution.
It provides the high-level status information in a user-friendly text.
The Method SelectJoint is used to select the joint and the associated joining process.

With this argument the Client can provide the identifier of the asset on which this method is applicable. It can be empty if the method is modelled directly under the required asset. If it is empty, the system can consider the identifier of the asset where the Server is running.
With this argument the Client can provide the identifier of the joint which should be selected for performing the next joining operation.

With this argument the Client can provide the common identifier of the joint which should be selected for performing the next joining operation.
It is optional and can be empty if the underlying system does not manage revisions of a joint. If JointId is provided, then this argument shall be ignored.
It provides the status of the Method execution.
It provides the high-level status information in a user-friendly text.
The Method SendJoint is used to send a joint to a joining system. If the input joint already exists in the system, it shall be overwritten.

With this argument the Client can provide the identifier of the asset on which this method is applicable. It can be empty if the method is modelled directly under the required asset. If it is empty, the system can consider the identifier of the asset where the Server is running.
With this argument the Client can provide the content of the joint.
It provides the status of the Method execution.
It provides the high-level status information in a user-friendly text.
The Method SendJointComponent is used to send a joint component to a joining system. If the input joint component already exists in the system, it shall be overwritten.

With this argument the Client can provide the joint component.
It provides the status of the Method execution.
It provides the high-level status information in a user-friendly text.
The Method SendJointDesign is used to send a joint design to a joining system. If the input joint design already exists in the system, it shall be overwritten.

With this argument the Client can provide the content of the joint design.
It provides the status of the Method execution.
It provides the high-level status information in a user-friendly text.
The MachineryBuildingBlocks contains building blocks from OPC UA for Machinery.
The ResultManagement Object is an instance of JoiningSystemResultManagementType which provides mechanisms to access results generated by the joining system.

With this argument the client can give a hint to the server how long it will need access to the result data. A value > 0 indicates an estimated maximum time for processing the data in milliseconds. A value = 0 indicates that the client will not need anything besides the data returned by the method call. A value < 0 indicates that the client cannot give an estimate. The client cannot rely on the data being available during the indicated time period. The argument is merely a hint allowing the server to optimize its resource management.

The server shall return to each client requesting result data a system-wide unique handle identifying the result set / client combination. This handle should be used by the client to indicate to the server that the result data is no longer needed, allowing the server to optimize its resource handling. If the instance of ResultManagementType does not support the ReleaseResultHandle Method, the resultHandle should always be set to 0. If the error is set to a value other than 0, the resultHandle may be set to 0.
The result including metadata.
0 – OK Values > 0 are reserved for errors defined by this and future standards. Values < 0 shall be used for application-specific errors.

The server shall return to each client requesting result data a system-wide unique handle identifying the result set / client combination. This handle should be used by the client to indicate to the server that the result data is no longer needed, allowing the server to optimize its resource handling. If the instance of ResultManagementType does not support the ReleaseResultHandle Method, the resultHandle should always be set to 0. If the error is set to a value other than 0, the resultHandle may be set to 0.
System-wide unique identifier for the result.

With this argument the client can give a hint to the server how long it will need access to the result data. A value > 0 indicates an estimated maximum time for processing the data in milliseconds. A value = 0 indicates that the client will not need anything besides the data returned by the method call. A value < 0 indicates that the client cannot give an estimate. The client cannot rely on the data being available during the indicated time period. The argument is merely a hint allowing the server to optimize its resource management.

The server shall return to each client requesting result data a system-wide unique handle identifying the result set / client combination. This handle should be used by the client to indicate to the server that the result data is no longer needed, allowing the server to optimize its resource handling. If the instance of ResultManagementType does not support the ReleaseResultHandle Method, the resultHandle should always be set to 0. If the error is set to a value other than 0, the resultHandle may be set to 0.
The result including metadata. May be set to Null, if error is set to a value other than 0.
0 – OK Values > 0 are reserved for errors defined by this and future standards. Values < 0 shall be used for application-specific errors.

Filter used to filter for specific results based on the meta data of the results. Valid BrowsePaths used in the filter can be built from the fields of the ResultReadyEventType, the ResultType VariableType or the ResultDataType or corresponding subtypes.

An array of BrowsePaths (as array of QualifiedName) identifying the ordering criteria for the results. If the array is null or empty, no ordering is executed. If several BrowsePaths are provided, the first entry in the array is used as first ordering criteria, etc.
Defines how many resultIds the Client wants to receive at most. If no maximum should be provided, it is set to 0.

With this argument the client can give a hint to the server how long it will need access to the result data. A value > 0 indicates an estimated maximum time for processing the data in milliseconds. A value = 0 indicates that the client will not need anything besides the data returned by the method call. A value < 0 indicates that the client cannot give an estimate. The client cannot rely on the data being available during the indicated time period. The argument is merely a hint allowing the server to optimize its resource management.

The server shall return to each client requesting result data a system-wide unique handle identifying the result set / client combination. This handle has to be used by the client to release the result set. If the instance of ResultManagementType does not support the ReleaseResultHandle Method, the resultHandle should always be set to 0. If the error is set to a value other than 0, the resultHandle may be set to 0.
List of resultIds of results matching the Filter.
0 – OK Values > 0 are reserved for errors defined by this and future standards. Values < 0 shall be used for application-specific errors.
Handle returned by GetResultById or GetResultIdListFiltered, identifying the result set/client combination.
0 – OK Values > 0 are reserved for errors defined by this and future standards. Values < 0 shall be used for application-specific errors.
The JointManagementType provides access to the Joint and associated information.
The default BrowseName for instances of the type.

It provides the high-level status information in a user-friendly text.
The Method GetJointComponentList is used to get the list of available joint components in the system.

With this argument the Client can provide the identifier of the asset on which this method is applicable. It can be empty if the method is modelled directly under the required asset. If it is empty, the system can consider the identifier of the asset where the Server is running.
It is the list of joint components available in the system.
It provides the status of the Method execution.
It provides the high-level status information in a user-friendly text.
The Method GetJointDesign is used to get the joint design based on the input identifier.

With this argument the Client can provide the identifier of the asset on which this method is applicable. It can be empty if the method is modelled directly under the required asset. If it is empty, the system can consider the identifier of the asset where the Server is running.
It is the origin identifier of the joint which is used to manage the revisions of a given joint.
It is the list of joints available in the system.

The Method SelectJoint is used to select the joint and the associated joining process.

With this argument the Client can provide the identifier of the asset on which this method is applicable. It can be empty if the method is modelled directly under the required asset. If it is empty, the system can consider the identifier of the asset where the Server is running.
With this argument the Client can provide the identifier of the joint which should be selected for performing the next joining operation.

With this argument the Client can provide the common identifier of the joint which should be selected for performing the next joining operation.
It is optional and can be empty if the underlying system does not manage revisions of a joint. If JointId is provided, then this argument shall be ignored.
It provides the status of the Method execution.
It provides the high-level status information in a user-friendly text.
The Method SendJoint is used to send a joint to a joining system. If the input joint already exists in the system, it shall be overwritten.

With this argument the Client can provide the identifier of the asset on which this method is applicable. It can be empty if the method is modelled directly under the required asset. If it is empty, the system can consider the identifier of the asset where the Server is running.

The JoiningSystemResultManagementType is a subtype of ResultManagementType and provides mechanism to access results generated by the underlying joining system.

System-wide unique identifier, which is assigned by the system. This ID can be used for fetching exactly this result using the method GetResultById and it is identical to the ResultId of the ResultReadyEventType. If the system does not manage resultIds, it should always be set to “NA”.

System-wide unique identifier, which is assigned by the system. This ID can be used for fetching exactly this result using the method GetResultById and it is identical to the ResultId of the ResultReadyEventType. If the system does not manage resultIds, it should always be set to “NA”.

The starting sequence number for the Requested Results.
It is a required argument if results are requested based on the input sequence number range.
It shall be a valid value > 0. If 0, then fromTime and toTime are used.

The ending sequence number of the Requested Results.
It is a required argument if results are requested based on the input sequence number range.
It shall be a valid value > 0 and shall be >= fromSequenceNumber. If 0, then fromTime and toTime are used.

It is the start time for the Requested Results.
This argument is considered only when fromSequenceNumber and toSequenceNumber are set as 0.

It is the end time for the Requested Results.
This argument is considered only when fromSequenceNumber and toSequenceNumber are set as 0.

The client can use this argument to configure a time interval between each Result to optimize the number of Results sent from the Server.
The Server can return the revised interval if the requested interval is not supported.
Note: It is only a requested minimum time interval by the client and the server could take additional time for processing.
It is the minimum revised interval supported by the server.
It provides the status of the Method execution.
It provides the high-level status information in a user-friendly text.

It is the maximum results requested by the Client.
If 0, then the Server shall send all the unacknowledged results.

The client can use this argument to configure a time interval between each Result to optimize the number of Results sent from the Server.
The Server can return the revised interval if the requested interval is not supported.
Note: It is only a requested minimum time interval by the client and the server could take additional time for processing.
It is the minimum revised interval supported by the server.
It is the total count of unacknowledged results in the server.
It provides the status of the Method execution.
It provides the high-level status information in a user-friendly text.
